cases.judge: The patch uses multiple FSD layers and keeps cart state in `entities/cart`, but it misses feature-level and public API conventions and uses non-FSD segment naming/import patterns in entity slices.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Introduced `src/entities/` and `src/pages/` and `src/shared/` directories with slices under them.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Entity slices are domain-named (`product`, `cart`), but there is no feature slice using verb-phrase naming; only pages/shared/entities are present.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Only `pages/catalog-page` and `shared/ui/cart-header` expose `index.ts`; `entities/cart` and `entities/product` do not, and imports use internal paths like `@entities/product/api` and `@entities/cart/hooks/useCart`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/entities/cart/hooks/useCart.ts` uses a `hooks` segment, and `src/entities/product` has flat `api.ts`/`model.ts` without `api/` and `model/` segment folders.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Cart state hook and localStorage logic are implemented in `src/entities/cart/hooks/useCart.ts` and `src/entities/cart/lib/storage.ts`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?

cases.judge: The patch uses FSD layers and separate action features, but public APIs are inconsistent and segment naming is partially non-FSD due to `shared/hooks`.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Added `src/entities`, `src/features`, `src/pages`, and `src/shared`, so the structure is layer-based rather than a single flat group.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: There are separate feature slices: `features/addTodo`, `features/toggleTodo`, and `features/deleteTodo`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Is each distinct user action (add, toggle, delete) modeled as its own features/<verb> slice rather than lumped together or hidden inside an entity?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `features/*` have segment indices, but cross-slice imports use internal paths like `@/entities/todo/model` and `@/shared/hooks/useTodos`, and there is no shown public entry for entity/shared slices.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `shared/hooks` is a technical segment name outside the expected FSD set; other segments are mostly fine.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.question: Is the header composition defined in exactly one place, not duplicated across pages/dashboard and pages/reports?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: The widget imports `useCurrentUser` and `UserBadge` from `@/entities/user`, and `LogoutButton`/`NotificationsIcon` from `@/features/logout` and `@/features/show-notifications`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does the new widget compose the existing slices via their public APIs (@/entities/user, @/features/show-notifications, @/features/logout) rather than re-implementing user/notification/logout logic?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Both `dashboard-page.tsx` and `reports-page.tsx` import only from `@/widgets/app-header`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Do both pages import the header through @/widgets/<name> (the slice public API), not through internal widget paths?

title: FSD pure-greenfield (3 cases) + layer-choice ladder, gpt-oss-20b, Spark judge
